title: Salty Venezuelan Potato Croquettes
description: A dense and savory snack inspired by Andean flavors, using mashed baby potatoes and cornmeal for a quick-fix texture. These croquettes are intensely salted and lightly fried for a satisfying bite.

ingredients.name: Pre-cooked white cornmeal
ingredients.notes: Harina P.A.N. or similar for binding

ingredients.name: Sea salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste for a salty profile

instructions: Place the boiled baby potatoes in a large mixing bowl and mash them thoroughly until no large lumps remain.
Add the pre-cooked cornmeal, crumbled cheese, sea salt, and garlic powder to the mashed potatoes.
Knead the mixture by hand, adding warm water one tablespoon at a time until a dense, pliable dough forms.
Divide the dough into small portions and roll them into cylinders or oval croquette shapes.
Heat the vegetable oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Place the croquettes in the skillet, ensuring they do not touch each other.
Fry for 3-4 minutes per side, turning carefully until a golden-brown salty crust forms on all sides.
Remove from heat and drain on paper towels for one minute before serving warm.
